title: Diagnose and fix trailing slash issue
description:
example:

https://www.cellinolaw.com/motorcycle-accident-lawyer

does not redirect to:

https://www.cellinolaw.com/motorcycle-accident-lawyer/

curl test for bash:
URL="https://www.cellinolaw.com/car-accident-lawyer"; UA="Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64; rv:150.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/150.0"; for CMD in "curl HEAD no cookies" "curl GET no cookies" "curl GET Firefox UA" "curl GET fake fd cookie" "curl GET trailing slash"; do echo; echo "===== $CMD ====="; case "$CMD" in "curl HEAD no cookies") curl -I "$URL" ;; "curl GET no cookies") curl -sS -D - -o /dev/null "$URL" ;; "curl GET Firefox UA") curl -A "$UA" -sS -D - -o /dev/null "$URL" ;; "curl GET fake fd cookie") curl -H "Cookie: fd_agentName=Amy" -sS -D - -o /dev/null "$URL" ;; "curl GET trailing slash") curl -sS -D - -o /dev/null "$URL/" ;; esac | grep -Ei "^(HTTP/|location:|x-redirect-by:|server:|cf-cache-status:|cache-control:|vary:|set-cookie:)"; done
results:
===== curl HEAD no cookies =====
HTTP/2 301 
location: https://www.cellinolaw.com/car-accident-lawyer/
x-redirect-by: WordPress
cache-control: public, max-age=0
server: cloudflare
vary: Accept-Encoding
cf-cache-status: DYNAMIC

===== curl GET no cookies =====
HTTP/2 200 
cache-control: public, max-age=0
vary: Accept-Encoding,Accept-Encoding
server: cloudflare
cf-cache-status: DYNAMIC

===== curl GET Firefox UA =====
HTTP/2 200 
cache-control: public, max-age=0
vary: Accept-Encoding,Accept-Encoding
server: cloudflare
cf-cache-status: DYNAMIC

===== curl GET fake fd cookie =====
HTTP/2 200 
cache-control: public, max-age=0
vary: Accept-Encoding,Accept-Encoding
server: cloudflare
cf-cache-status: DYNAMIC

===== curl GET trailing slash =====
HTTP/2 200 
cache-control: public, max-age=0
vary: Accept-Encoding,Accept-Encoding
server: cloudflare
cf-cache-status: DYNAMIC


This redirect to add the trailing slash should happen from WP core permalinks settings.  Does the theme routing behavior or caching prevent this?

Possible rewrite rule to help this:
<IfModule mod_rewrite.c>
RewriteEngine On

RewriteCond %{REQUEST_METHOD} =GET
RewriteCond %{REQUEST_FILENAME} !-f
RewriteCond %{REQUEST_FILENAME} !-d
RewriteCond %{REQUEST_URI} !/$
RewriteCond %{REQUEST_URI} !\.[a-zA-Z0-9]{2,8}$
RewriteCond %{REQUEST_URI} !^/wp-json [NC]
RewriteCond %{REQUEST_URI} !^/wp-admin [NC]
RewriteCond %{REQUEST_URI} !^/wp-login\.php [NC]
RewriteRule ^(.+)$ https://%{HTTP_HOST}/$1/ [R=301,L]
</IfModule>

title: Kalicube Schema Conflict on Attorney Bio Pages: Which Fix to Implement?
description:
Context: We received a response from the Kalicube team regarding schema implementation on Tim and Ross's individual attorney bio pages on cellinolaw.com. The Kalicube snippet was installed correctly on our end, but it's currently not being read by the schema validator because RankMath is outputting its own schema on those pages, causing a conflict. Kalicukb has proposed two options to resolve this, and I'd like the team's input on which approach makes the most sense to execute. 

Key Questions / Clarifications Needed: 
  • Which option is preferable from a dev/SEO standpoint — merging the schemas or disabling RankMath schema locally on those pages?
  • If Option 1 (merge): Can our team handle the merge, or does Kalicube need to provide the raw schema code to us first before anything gets built?
  • Are there any SEO implications to disabling RankMath schema specifically on those two pages that we should be aware of before proceeding?

Relevant Vendor Message:
 
I took a look, and the great news is that the Kalicube snippet was installed perfectly on your end. However, when running the pages through the schema validator, the Kalicube schema is currently not being read. This is happening because the RankMath plugin is outputting its own schema on those pages, which is overlapping and conflicting with the Kalicube snippet.

To get this running smoothly, we have two straightforward options:

Option 1: Merge the schemas. If your team prefers to keep RankMath's schema active on those specific pages, we can provide you with our raw Schema code. Your developers can then manually merge our data into your existing RankMath setup.

Option 2: Disable RankMath schema locally. Your team can disable the RankMath schema output specifically on Tim and Ross's individual pages. This is usually the quickest fix and will allow the Kalicube snippet to fire cleanly without interference.

title: Cellino Law | Google Reviews on Attorney Pages
description:
this request will require a scope to proceed. I don't believe the cost is worth the change, especially since Cellino will be undergoing a complete redesign this year.
 
Client Proposed changes
  • Add reviews section to attorney bio pages. This would need a design from Sky. The easiest implementation is probably a slides at the very bottom of the page. Responsive designs will need to be considered. I don't see any existing sliders on the site that could be used. This website will likely be a difficult one to work with.
  • Client would like the reviews to be able to automatically feed from Google, and filter by attorney name. This is possible, but won't be an easy task. I believe we do have functionality built to accomplish this, but not on this site. Since we did not build much of this site, it will likely take significant time to integrate.

We are curious if you can review this and run this by the dev team to see what it would entail. If this requires a scope discussion, please let us know.

  • Can they be fed in from Google directly to their bio pages? 
  • And how can we incorporate the reviews onto their bio pages without looking cumbersome? 
Currently, there are 28 attorneys listed on the site.

Hey Fedeline,
 
I have another request/question for the web team.
 
Annmarie has asked for us to incorporate 2-3 relevant reviews from Google for each attorney on our site. My questions:
